title: linaria supina var. maritima
intro: Linaria supina var. maritima, also known as sea toadflax, is a perennial plant native to the coastal regions of Europe and Western Asia, characterized by its yellow or purple flowers and blue-green foliage. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal effects. The health benefits of Linaria supina var. maritima include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and boost the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, although its use should be approached with caution due to potential side effects and interactions with other medications.
